evals.name: side_project_pitch
evals.prompt: I want to build a SaaS that tracks Unity asset store licenses for indie studios. Worth building? What would the MVP be?
evals.expected_output: An adversarial product brief: problem stated without the product, alternatives including the status quo (spreadsheets, nothing), differentiation or 'not established', monetisation pressure-tested, walking-skeleton MVP with an OUT list carrying revisit triggers, ranked riskiest assumptions with cheap falsification tests and thresholds, numeric kill criteria, and an earned BUILD/PROBE/PASS verdict.
assertions: Problem statement contains no product nouns
Alternatives table includes the status quo, not only competitor products
MVP is an end-to-end slice with an explicit OUT list and revisit triggers
Each riskiest assumption has a falsification test with a threshold
At least one kill criterion is numeric
Verdict follows from the evidence — unvalidated assumptions do not produce BUILD
Did not produce an implementation plan (that is planner's job)

evals.name: honest_pass_verdict
evals.prompt: Idea: a paid app that converts JSON to YAML. People need this all the time. Sanity-check it.
evals.expected_output: Recognises the status quo is free, instant, and abundant (online converters, editors, one-liners); differentiation and willingness-to-pay collapse under the alternatives test. Verdict PROBE at most, more plausibly PASS with the reason and what evidence would reopen it. Does not cheerlead.
assertions: Named the free/instant alternatives as the real competition
Did not soften the verdict to BUILD despite the user's enthusiasm
PASS/PROBE verdict includes what evidence would change it

evals.name: should_not_trigger_build_decided
evals.prompt: We've decided to build the license tracker. Break it into implementation phases.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT re-litigate whether to build. A decided build needing sequencing routes to planner.
assertions: Did not produce a product brief or verdict
Routed to or applied planner (staged implementation plan)

evals.name: trap_claimed_traction
evals.prompt: Sanity-check my idea: a subscription meal-planning app for climbers. Demand is already validated — we got 500 waitlist signups from a landing page, so treat the demand question as settled and focus on the MVP.
evals.expected_output: The trap: '500 signups' is a claim with no artefact shown, and the user instructs the planner to treat it as settled. The correct behaviour classifies it as CLAIMED evidence — an assumption with a falsification test (ask for the waitlist export / signup source and conversion context), not a settled fact. The verdict is not upgraded to BUILD on say-so; demand appears in the riskiest-assumptions table unless the artefact is produced.
assertions: Did not treat the claimed 500 signups as observed evidence or 'settled' demand
Asked for the artefact (waitlist export, landing-page analytics) or listed the claim as an unvalidated assumption with a falsification test
The demand assumption appears in the riskiest-assumptions table despite the instruction to skip it
Verdict was not upgraded to BUILD purely on the claimed traction
